title: Multivariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine
text: component multivariate normal conjugate Normal-Inverse-Wishart cluster base measure (dmnorm(cov=) + dinvwish), data-scaled default. NIMBLE’s native conjugate CRP samplers used – nimix deliberately reimplement .

title: Multivariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine
text: 2022 data dominant structure clearly separated high-income / high-longevity cluster plus overlapping developing-country clusters: Overlapping components small weights expected DPM; report dominant components , hard partition needed, use MAP allocation:
code: fit <- nimixClust(Y, K_max = 8,                   mcmcControl = list(niter = 4000, nburnin = 1500),                   seed = 1) fit <- relabel(fit) summary(fit) #> modalK = 4 #>   component weight   mu_1   mu_2      # (log GDP pc, life expectancy) #> 3         3  0.309 10.363 80.302     # rich, long-lived cluster #> 4         4  0.444  8.087 69.402     # large developing cluster #> 1         1  0.204  8.570 71.788     # overlapping middle cluster #> 2         2  0.043  8.556 69.914     # small transient component alloc <- apply(fit@clusterAllocation, 2L, function(z)   as.integer(names(which.max(table(z))))) plot(Y, col = alloc, pch = 19,      main = "MAP cluster allocation")

title: Multivariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine
code: fit3 <- nimixClust(Y, K = 3, method = "fixedk",                    mcmcControl = list(niter = 4000, nburnin = 1500),                    seed = 1) summary(relabel(fit3))

Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   5.574   7.766   8.806   8.893   9.984  12.275 hist(ly, breaks = 30, main = "log GDP per capita, 2022", xlab = "log US$")

title: Univariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine
text: method = "dpm" estimates number occupied components. K_max truncation level (computational headroom), prior belief K. 2022 data finds two-regime structure: posterior number clusters concentrates 2-3, usual DPM tail small transient components. fit warns truncation level reached non-negligible share iterations, increase K_max re-run – larger truncation adds headroom.
code: fit <- nimixClust(ly, K_max = 10,                   mcmcControl = list(niter = 4000, nburnin = 1500),                   seed = 1) fit <- relabel(fit)   # always relabel before summarising (label switching) summary(fit) #> modalK = 2 #>   weight mu_mean #> 1  0.554   7.966    # ~ US$ 2,900  (lower/middle-income regime) #> 2  0.446   9.964    # ~ US$ 21,000 (high-income regime)

title: Univariate mixture clustering with the DPM engine
text: Multiple chains dispersed starts cost almost nothing extra: compiled model reused across chains, summary() reports cross-chain split-Rhat / ESS label-invariant quantities.
code: fit3 <- nimixClust(ly, K_max = 10,                    mcmcControl = list(niter = 4000, nburnin = 1500,                                       nchains = 3),                    seed = 1) summary(relabel(fit3))

title: Mixtures of linear regressions
code: library(nimix) #> Loading required package: nimble #> nimble version 1.4.2 is loaded. #> For more information on NIMBLE and a User Manual, #> please visit https://R-nimble.org. #>  #> Attaching package: 'nimble' #> The following object is masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     simulate #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     declare data(wdi2022) df <- data.frame(life = wdi2022$life_exp,                  lgdp = log(wdi2022$gdp_pc)) plot(df$lgdp, df$life, xlab = "log GDP per capita",      ylab = "life expectancy", main = "Preston curve, 2022")

what: Does the Preston curve have latent regimes?
title: Mixtures of linear regressions
text: instructive result: log income DPM concentrates single regime – roughly four extra years life expectancy per log-dollar income. lesson nonparametric mixture invent regimes: one regression line (residual spread) explains data, posterior collapses . Estimated K always depends assumed component form – heterogeneity lives residuals, distinct slopes. Even two components offered (fixed K = 2), one simply empties:
code: fit <- nimixReg(life ~ lgdp, df, K_max = 6, method = "dpm",                 mcmcControl = list(niter = 5000, nburnin = 2000),                 seed = 1) fit <- relabel(fit) summary(fit) #> modalK = 1 #>   (Intercept)  lgdp #> 1      36.782 4.067      # life ~ 36.8 + 4.07 x log GDP pc fit2 <- nimixReg(life ~ lgdp, df, K = 2, method = "fixedk",                  mcmcControl = list(niter = 5000, nburnin = 2000),                  seed = 1) summary(relabel(fit2)) #> one occupied component; weight ~ 1 on the Preston line

title: Mixtures of linear regressions
text: genuinely regime-structured data call recovers . simulated benchmark (two latent groups slopes +2 / -2, mixed 80/20):
code: set.seed(107) n <- 250; x <- runif(n, -3, 3) grp <- rep(1:2, c(200, 50)) y <- ifelse(grp == 1, 2 * x, -2 * x) + rnorm(n, 0, 0.7) fs <- nimixReg(y ~ x, data.frame(y = y, x = x), K_max = 8,                mcmcControl = list(niter = 5000, nburnin = 2000), seed = 1) summary(relabel(fs))    # recovers slopes near +2 and -2, weights near 0.8/0.2

title: Mixtures of linear regressions
text: Heavy-tailed residuals (distribution = "studentt" / "normalgamma") multivariate responses (cbind(y1, y2) ~ x) use interface.
code: newd <- data.frame(lgdp = log(c(1000, 10000, 50000))) predict(fit, newdata = newd)

title: Heavy tails: Student-t vs Normal-Gamma components
code: library(nimix) #> Loading required package: nimble #> nimble version 1.4.2 is loaded. #> For more information on NIMBLE and a User Manual, #> please visit https://R-nimble.org. #>  #> Attaching package: 'nimble' #> The following object is masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     simulate #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     declare data(wdi2022) ly <- log(wdi2022$gdp_pc)

title: Heavy tails: Student-t vs Normal-Gamma components
text: Actual results dataset: agree two dominant income regimes. augmented path found one extra small component mixed somewhat slowly partition.
code: ctrl <- list(niter = 4000, nburnin = 1500) ft <- relabel(nimixClust(ly, K_max = 8, distribution = "studentt",                          mcmcControl = ctrl, seed = 1)) fg <- relabel(nimixClust(ly, K_max = 8, distribution = "normalgamma",                          mcmcControl = ctrl, seed = 1)) summary(ft); summary(fg) #> studentt     : modalK = 2, regimes at log-GDP 8.00 / 10.06, #>                ESS(#clusters) = 147, ~30 s #> normalgamma  : modalK = 3, dominant regimes 8.07 / 10.10 (+ small 10% #>                middle component), ESS(#clusters) = 116, ~38 s

title: Heavy tails: Student-t vs Normal-Gamma components
text: augmentation restores conjugacy, per-observation omega_i couple tightly partition, can slow mixing number clusters – penalty found consistently benchmarks mitigate replacing default random-walk sampler omega slice sampling (done automatically via spec’s sampler hook). Practical guidance: "studentt" sensible default heavy-tailed clustering; "normalgamma" worth trying Gibbs updates advantageous (e.g. larger n per component), omega_i posteriors useful: small values flag observations model treats outliers (robustness-weight interpretation). Always compare summary() ESS diagnostics data rather assuming one uniformly better.

code: library(nimix) #> Loading required package: nimble #> nimble version 1.4.2 is loaded. #> For more information on NIMBLE and a User Manual, #> please visit https://R-nimble.org. #>  #> Attaching package: 'nimble' #> The following object is masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     simulate #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     declare data(usStates2023); data(usStateAdj) sw <- spatialWeights(usStateAdj) sw #> SpatialWeightSpec: 49 regions, 112 undirected edges #>   degree range: 1 - 8 | weights: binary (contiguity) neighborsOf(sw, "TN")     # Tennessee's famous 8 neighbours #> [1] "AL" "AR" "GA" "KY" "MS" "MO" "NC" "VA" summary(usStates2023$povertyRate) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>    7.30   10.30   11.90   12.27   13.70   18.90

title: Spatially constrained mixtures (MRF engine)
text: Potts normalising constant intractable. fixed beta (default prior$beta = 0.8) constant absorbed MCMC labels component parameters exact. Estimating beta (prior$estimateBeta = TRUE) uses classical pseudo-likelihood Metropolis update (Besag 1975) – approximation, slightly biased strong interactions; exchange-algorithm refinement possible future upgrade.

what: Two poverty regimes with spatial cohesion
title: Spatially constrained mixtures (MRF engine)
text: Actual results 2023 data: high-poverty regime spatially contiguous Southern belt (plus New Mexico, DC enclave inside belt) – exactly kind structure exchangeable mixture represent. fixedk fit data finds similar regime means agrees MRF allocation 82% states: spatial smoothing snaps boundary states neighbours’ regime.
code: y <- usStates2023$povertyRate fit <- nimixClust(y, K = 2, method = "mrf", spatialWeights = sw,                   prior = list(estimateBeta = TRUE),                   mcmcControl = list(niter = 4000, nburnin = 1500),                   seed = 1) fit <- relabel(fit) summary(fit) #> regimes: 11.4% vs 14.5% poverty (weights 0.73 / 0.27) #> beta   : posterior mean 1.22, P(beta > 0.2) = 1  (clear positive interaction) #> high-poverty regime (13 regions): #>   AL AR DC FL GA LA MS NC NM OK SC TN TX zMap <- apply(fit@clusterAllocation, 2L,               function(v) as.integer(names(which.max(table(v))))) split(usStates2023$postal, zMap)

title: Spatially constrained mixtures (MRF engine)
text: beta = 0 removes smoothing (labels independent given components); larger beta increases cohesion. estimated, summary() reports split-Rhat / ESS posterior mean beta. engine warns posterior beta piles near upper prior bound prior$betaMax (default 2): (near-)saturated field – raise betaMax interpret smoothing effectively maximal.

title: Spatially constrained mixtures (MRF engine)
text: field applies nimixReg(): regression coefficients cluster across adjacent regions (e.g. growth patterns across neighbouring areas). state-level pair fit collapses single national regime (pov ~ 144.6 - 11.8 log(income), strong income-poverty gradient): 49 regions one dominant relationship, model invent second regime – honesty Preston-curve vignette shows exchangeable case.
code: ## regimes in how income relates to poverty across neighbouring states df <- data.frame(pov = usStates2023$povertyRate,                  inc = log(usStates2023$medianIncome)) fr <- nimixReg(pov ~ inc, df, K = 2, method = "mrf", spatialWeights = sw,                prior = list(estimateBeta = TRUE),                mcmcControl = list(niter = 4000, nburnin = 1500), seed = 1) summary(relabel(fr))

title: Spatially constrained mixtures (MRF engine)
text: Estimated beta pseudo-likelihood approximation (see ); MRF engine currently supports Gaussian components (univariate, multivariate, univariate-response regression); heavy-tailed components spatial engine planned. Synthetic-lattice recovery benchmarks three model types ship inst/harness/run_recovery_suite.R (groups mrf, mrfmv, mrfreg).

what: Bayesian Mixture Clustering and Regression with NIMBLE
title: Bayesian Mixture Clustering and Regression with NIMBLE
text: nimix Bayesian mixture-modelling package built top NIMBLE. provides mixture clustering (univariate multivariate) mixtures regressions (univariate multivariate response) extensible S4 DistributionSpec contract, new component families plug every inference engine without touching engine code.

title: Bayesian Mixture Clustering and Regression with NIMBLE
text: Clustering — univariate multivariate Gaussian, plus Student-t, Normal-Gamma (heavy-tailed), Poisson Binomial components. Mixture regressions (nimixReg) — Gaussian linear, Student-t Normal-Gamma residuals, Poisson/Binomial GLM components, multivariate (matrix) response via cbind(y1, y2) ~ x. "dpm" — Dirichlet Process Mixture infers number components (NIMBLE’s Chinese Restaurant Process), using K_max. "fixedk" — finite mixture fixed, known K (including single-component K = 1 baseline model comparison). Post-hoc label-switching correction via label.switching package, summary(), plot() predict() methods.

title: Bayesian Mixture Clustering and Regression with NIMBLE
text: nimix depends nimble, compiles model code, need working C/C++ toolchain (Rtools Windows, Xcode CLT macOS, build-essential Linux).

title: Bayesian Mixture Clustering and Regression with NIMBLE
code: library(nimix)  ## Univariate clustering, number of components estimated (DPM) set.seed(1) y <- c(rnorm(100, -3), rnorm(100, 3)) fit <- nimixClust(y, K_max = 8,                   mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                   verbose = FALSE) summary(fit) plot(fit, type = "K")  ## Mixture of linear regressions, fixed number of regimes (finite mixture) x  <- runif(200, -3, 3) g  <- rep(1:2, each = 100) df <- data.frame(y = ifelse(g == 1, 2 * x, -2 * x) + rnorm(200, 0, 0.7), x = x) reg <- nimixReg(y ~ x, df, K = 2, method = "fixedk",                 mcmcControl = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 1000),                 verbose = FALSE) summary(reg)

title: Fitted nimix mixture result — FitResult-class
text: object FitResult. ... Passed relabel relabelling triggered. x FitResult. y Ignored. type One "K" (posterior #clusters), "trace_raw" (raw cluster-parameter traces; zig-zags reveal label switching), "trace_relabeled" (traces relabelling), "density" (univariate clustering : data histogram posterior predictive overlay), "cluster" (multivariate clustering, \(d \ge 2\): scatter coloured MAP cluster), "fitted" (regression : observed response vs posterior predictive mean). newdata Points evaluate: numeric vector (univariate clustering), matrix d columns (multivariate clustering), data frame predictors (regression). Defaults training data. maxDraws Integer cap number posterior draws used (default 500); draws thinned uniformly exceeded.

title: Fitted nimix mixture result — FitResult-class
text: show(FitResult): Compact display fitted result. summary(FitResult): Posterior summary (relabelled component estimates, posterior number clusters, mixing diagnostic). plot(x = FitResult, y = missing): Diagnostic result plots. predict(FitResult): Posterior predictive density new points. Returns posterior predictive density averaged MCMC draws, using occupied-cluster mixture draw (weights = cluster sizes / n). quantity label-invariant, relabelling required. multivariate fits density evaluated supplied rows newdata. regression fit (nimixReg) instead returns posterior predictive mean \(E[y \mid x]\) per row newdata (column .fitted). keep evaluation tractable draws subsampled maxDraws.

title: Fitted nimix mixture result — FitResult-class
text: mcmcSamples matrix monitored MCMC draws (iterations x parameters). Kposterior Integer vector: number occupied clusters per iteration. clusterAllocation Integer matrix (iterations x n) raw cluster indicators xi. paramTrace named list raw cluster-parameter traces (iterations x Kmax matrix). engineUsed Character scalar naming engine. distSpec DistributionSpec used. data data model fit . Kmax Integer truncation level. prior prior list used. relabeled list cache populated relabel (empty). mcmcControl MCMC control list actually used. diagnostics Multi-chain convergence diagnostics (Rhat, ESS) one chain run; otherwise single-chain summary. call matched call.

what: Finite-mixture engine with fixed, known K — FixedKEngine-class
title: Finite-mixture engine with fixed, known K — FixedKEngine-class
text: simplest engine: number components K fixed (inferred). Mixing weights symmetric Dirichlet prior observation categorical allocation. Chinese Restaurant Process, truncation considerations DPM apply, NIMBLE assigns conjugate samplers weights component parameters. Useful fast baseline K known assumed, classical model selection comparing fits across several values K.

title: Build the NIMBLE model code for a (distribution, engine) pair — buildModelCode
text: buildModelCode(spec = PoissonRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Poisson GLM regression DPM code (log link). buildModelCode(spec = PoissonRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Poisson GLM regression fixed-K code (log link). buildModelCode(spec = BinomialRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Binomial GLM regression DPM code (logit link). buildModelCode(spec = BinomialRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Binomial GLM regression fixed-K code (logit link). buildModelCode(spec = GMSNBurrUvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): GMSNBurr finite mixture (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = GMSNBurrUvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): GMSNBurr DPM mixture. buildModelCode(spec = NormalRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): DPM mixture--linear-regressions model code   (dCRP) conjugate Normal-Inverse-Gamma cluster prior constant   (CRP) gating. Builds NIMBLE code $$y_i \sim N(x_i^\top \beta_{\xi_i}, s^2_{\xi_i}), \quad       \xi_{1:n} \sim CRP(\alpha, n),$$ \(s^2_j \sim InvGamma(nu_0, s_0)\) \(\beta_j \mid s^2_j \sim N_p(b_0, s^2_j B_0)\). number predictors \(p\) design matrix \(X\) constants. buildModelCode(spec = StudentTRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Student-t regression DPM code (direct t density; scale enters precision tau = 1 / s2). buildModelCode(spec = StudentTRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Student-t regression fixed-K code. buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Normal-Gamma regression DPM code (scale mixture; conjugate coefficients conditional omega). buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Normal-Gamma regression fixed-K code. buildModelCode(spec = MSNBurrUvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): MSNBurr finite mixture (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = MSNBurr2aUvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): MSNBurr-IIa finite mixture (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = MSNBurrUvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): MSNBurr DPM mixture. buildModelCode(spec = MSNBurr2aUvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): MSNBurr-IIa DPM mixture. buildModelCode(spec = NormalMvRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate-response Normal regression DPM code. buildModelCode(spec = NormalMvRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate-response Normal regression fixed-K. buildModelCode(spec = StudentTMvRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate-response Student-t regression DPM code (direct multivariate-t kernel). buildModelCode(spec = StudentTMvRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate-response Student-t regression fixed-K. buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaMvRegSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate-response Normal-Gamma regression DPM code (scale mixture; conjugate cluster updates). buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaMvRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate-response Normal-Gamma regression fixed-K code. buildModelCode(spec = NormalMvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate Gaussian DPM model code (dCRP)   conjugate Normal-Inverse-Wishart cluster base measure. Builds NIMBLE code $$y_i \sim N_d(\mu_{\xi_i}, \Sigma_{\xi_i}), \quad       \xi_{1:n} \sim CRP(\alpha, n),$$ \(\Sigma_j \sim InvWishart(S_0, df_0)\) \(\mu_j \sim N_d(\mu_0, \Sigma_j / \kappa_0)\). dimension \(d\) constant index ranges 1:d expand model-build time. buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaMvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate Normal-Gamma scale-mixture DPM code per-observation latent precision multiplier omega. buildModelCode(spec = NormalUvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Univariate Gaussian DPM model code (dCRP). Builds NIMBLE code $$y_i \sim N(\mu_{\xi_i}, s^2_{\xi_i}), \quad       \xi_{1:n} \sim CRP(\alpha, n),$$ conjugate Normal-Inverse-Gamma cluster prior Gamma hyperprior \(\alpha\). Cluster-parameter vectors length L = K_max (NIMBLE's exact truncation; sampler stays proper long number occupied clusters strictly L). buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaUvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Normal-Gamma scale-mixture DPM model code (dCRP) per-observation latent precision multiplier omega. buildModelCode(spec = PoissonSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Poisson DPM model code (Gamma-Poisson conjugate). buildModelCode(spec = BinomialSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Binomial DPM model code (Beta-Binomial conjugate). buildModelCode(spec = StudentTMvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Multivariate Student-t DPM code using user-defined dmvt_nimix kernel Normal-Inverse-Wishart cluster prior. buildModelCode(spec = StudentTUvSpec, engine = DPMEngine): Univariate Student-t DPM model code (dCRP). t density evaluated directly; df constant. buildModelCode(spec = NormalUvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Univariate Gaussian finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = NormalMvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate Gaussian finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = NormalRegSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Mixture--linear-regressions finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = StudentTUvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Univariate Student-t finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaUvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Univariate Normal-Gamma (scale-mixture Student-t) finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = NormalGammaMvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate Normal-Gamma scale-mixture finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = StudentTMvSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Multivariate Student-t finite-mixture code (fixed K) using user-defined dmvt_nimix kernel. buildModelCode(spec = PoissonSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Poisson finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = BinomialSpec, engine = FixedKEngine): Binomial finite-mixture code (fixed K). buildModelCode(spec = DistributionSpec, engine = MRFEngine): Default MRF kernel family: family's fixed-K kernel Dirichlet-categorical label layer replaced joint Potts node (derived mechanically; title: GMSNBurr Distribution — gmsnburr-distribution
text: GMSNBurr distribution parameters \(\mu\), \(\sigma\), \(\alpha\), \(\theta\) probability density function: $$f(x|\mu,\sigma,\alpha,\theta) = \frac{\omega}{B(\alpha,\beta)\sigma}   \left(\frac{\theta}{\alpha}\right)^\theta   \exp\left(-\theta\omega\frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)   \left(1 + \frac{\theta}{\alpha}   \exp\left(-\omega\frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)\right)^{-(\alpha+\theta)}$$ Special cases: \(\alpha = \theta\): Symmetric distribution \(\alpha < \theta\): Left-skewed \(\alpha > \theta\): Right-skewed \(\theta = 1\): Reduces MSNBurr \(\alpha = 1\): Reduces MSNBurr-IIa \(\alpha = \theta \\infty\): Converges \(\mathcal{N}(\mu, \sigma^2)\)

title: MSNBurr Distribution — msnburr-distribution
text: MSNBurr distribution parameters \(\mu\), \(\sigma\), \(\alpha\) probability density function: $$f(x|\mu,\sigma,\alpha) = \frac{\omega}{\sigma}   \exp\left(-\omega\frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)   \left(1 + \frac{1}{\alpha}\exp\left(-\omega\frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)\right)^{-(\alpha+1)}$$ \(-\infty < x < \infty\), \(-\infty < \mu < \infty\), \(\sigma > 0\), \(\alpha > 0\), \(\omega\) normalizing constant: $$\omega = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}   \left(1 + \frac{1}{\alpha}\right)^{\alpha+1}$$ MSNBurr special case GMSNBurr distribution \(\theta = 1\). left-skewed (negative skewness).

title: Bayesian mixture clustering — nimixClust
text: data numeric vector (univariate) numeric matrix one row per observation one column per dimension (multivariate). single-column matrix treated univariate. K Integer number components finite mixture (method = "fixedk"). Required method; must given method = "dpm" (use K_max ). K_max Integer truncation level Dirichlet Process Mixture (method = "dpm"); number components estimated bound. dCRP sampler errors occupied-cluster count ever needs exceed , K_max sit comfortably expected number clusters; generous data-aware default (giving headroom count) used missing. Must given method = "fixedk" (use K). distribution Component distribution. "normal" (default) picks univariate multivariate Gaussian automatically data shape; "normal-uv" / "normal-mv" force specific one. Student-t / Poisson / Binomial planned v0.4.0. method Engine: "dpm" (default; estimate number components), "fixedk" (finite mixture known K), "mrf" (spatially constrained finite mixture: known K, Potts smoothing labels spatialWeights graph; univariate Gaussian components, fixed interaction prior$beta, default 0.8). prior named list prior overrides passed defaultPrior (univariate: cLoc, nu0; multivariate: cLoc, df0) plus, DPM, optional concPrior = c(shape, rate) concentration, , finite mixture, dirichletConc Dirichlet weight prior. mcmcControl named list MCMC controls: niter, nburnin, thin, optional initRatio – fraction truncation / component cap (K_max K) seeded dispersed cluster initialisation (default 0.8; must lie (0, 1)). Lower leave headroom truncation; raising 0.95 allowed warns, leaves little headroom – reuse (default TRUE): reuse compiled NIMBLE model later fit identical structure (generated code, constants, monitors component family), resetting data initial values. skips recompilation repeated fits (multiple seeds, multiple chains) bit--bit identical fresh compile. See nimixClearCache. Also nchains (default 1): run many chains dispersed, separately seeded starts (reusing compiled model, first chain compiles) report multi-chain split-Rhat effective sample size label-invariant quantities summary(). Set parallel = TRUE (nchains > 1) run chains parallel: worker compiles model separate directory (fork-safe way parallelise NIMBLE), using parallel::mclapply. requires forking platform (Unix/macOS, Windows, falls back sequential) trades compile-cache one compile per worker; ncores caps number workers (default detected cores). initMethod Initialisation cluster allocation: "kmeans" (default, dispersed start) "single". seed Integer RNG seed reproducibility. verbose Logical; print NIMBLE's configuration progress output. Defaults FALSE (quiet): NIMBLE's compilation notes benign dCRP truncation note silenced, nimix's diagnostics (e.g. censored-posterior warning) error still surface. Set TRUE see NIMBLE's configuration progress bar. spatialWeights Optional SpatialWeightSpec describing neighbourhood graph observations (one region per observation). Required , used , method = "mrf".

what: Cluster profiling, richer summaries, model selection and ensembling
title: nimix 1.0.0 (in development)
text: summary() relabelled clustering fit now reports posterior median alongside mean 95% credible interval every scalar component parameter (univariate families) per dimension multivariate clustering. New clusterProfile(): assigns observation MAP cluster describes observed data within cluster (size, proportion, per-variable mean / sd / median) – data-side complement summary() (reports fitted parameters) plot(fit, "cluster") (shows partition). New predictive model-selection layer built label-invariant pointwise mixture log-likelihood: nimixWAIC() (native; Watanabe 2010), nimixLOO() (PSIS-LOO via loo package; Vehtari, Gelman & Gabry 2017), modelSelect() rank several fits (e.g. choosing K, comparing Normal / Student-t / MSNBurr components data). New ensembleFit(): combines several fits one weighted predictive model via Bayesian stacking Pseudo-BMA+ (Yao et al. 2018, needs loo) Akaike-style WAIC weights (native). predict() returns ensemble-weighted density. loo added Suggests.

title: nimix 1.0.0 (in development)
text: Two new univariate component families, distribution = "msnburr" "msnburr2a" (Iriawan 2000; Choir 2020), location, scale, skewness shape alpha (alpha = 1 exactly logistic distribution; MSNBurr accommodates left skew, MSNBurr-IIa mirror). Available three engines: finite-K, DPM, spatial MRF. Numerically stable throughout, using maintainer-contributed reference implementation: asymptotic log-omega branch alpha -> 0, branch-free softplus compiled densities, two-branch quantile inversion. Log-densities stay finite standardized values hundreds; density integrates one alpha = 0.05 alpha = 100; NIMBLE R densities agree 1e-8; ppCheck() supports families. Verified recovery synthetic skew-component mixtures (finite-K allocation accuracy 1.00 / 0.99, DPM recovers true K, MRF accuracy 1.00).

what: Bayesian workflow: modern mixture-appropriate convergence + predictive checks
title: nimix 1.0.0 (in development)
text: Full Vehtari et al. (2021) convergence suite – rank-normalized split-Rhat (already present), folded split-Rhat, bulk-ESS tail-ESS – computed label-invariant functionals (occupied K, DPM alpha, MRF beta, new per-iteration allocation entropy): per-component traces identified label switching, chain diagnostics meaningless. summary() prints functional table documented Rhat < 1.01 aim; helpers unit-tested theory (iid ESS ~ n, folded Rhat detects pure scale disagreement location Rhat misses, AR(0.9) ESS matches analytic value). New ppCheck(): posterior predictive checks (Gelman, Meng & Stern 1996; Gelman et al. 2020, Bayesian workflow) replicates drawn conditionally draw’s fitted allocation – label-invariant construction valid every engine including spatial MRF. Built-statistics (mean, sd, min, max, skew) user functions; clustering families (Gaussian uv/mv, Student-t, Normal-Gamma, Poisson, Binomial) supported, regression checks planned. Verified pass well-specified Gaussian mixture flag Poisson mixture overdispersed counts (tail p = 0.013).

what: MRF engine across the distribution registry (batch 2) – matrix complete
title: nimix 0.9.0
text: method = "mrf" now covers every registered family: added multivariate Student-t clustering, multivariate-response regression (Gaussian, Student-t Normal-Gamma responses), augmented Normal-Gamma routes (univariate/multivariate clustering univariate regression), O(nK + edges) label sweep. Harness groups mrfdist2 mrfdist2reg pass synthetic lattice (7 combinations x 3 seeds). Critical correctness fix. hand-written MRF kernels Gaussian specs shadowed generic .pottsify() route heavy-tailed subclasses (S4 inheritance), e.g. Student-t regression mrf silently used Gaussian kernel parameter updates. hand-written kernels removed: every family’s MRF kernel now derived fixed-K kernel, regression test pins kernel/family correspondence. internal quiet-mode handler crashed NIMBLE conditions empty messages (“argument length zero”); now robust, benign “samplers assigned” notice deliberately unsampled fixed beta node muffled. Mixing benchmark (documented commitment): identical heavy-tailed lattice data, augmented Normal-Gamma route mixes component means BETTER direct Student-t density MRF (ESS minimum component mean 1357 vs 550 equal wall time) – reverse exchangeable-DPM finding, K fixed labels pinned spatial field omega-partition coupling penalty largely disappears conjugate Gibbs updates win. Guidance: prefer normalgamma MRF parameter mixing; direct-t route remains available equally valid.

what: MRF engine across the distribution registry (batch 1: closed-form emissions)
title: nimix 0.8.0
text: Per maintainer-approved feasibility study, method = "mrf" now covers six family/task combinations: Poisson Binomial clustering, Poisson-GLM (log link) Binomial-GLM (logit link) regression, direct Student-t clustering regression. Every DSL emission call verified empirically reference densities implementation. New internal .pottsify() transformation derives family’s MRF kernel mechanically fixed-K kernel (drop Dirichlet-categorical label layer, insert joint Potts node), default buildModelCode(<spec>, MRFEngine) now exists; three original hand-written MRF kernels unchanged. Label sweeps remain family-specific O(nK + edges) performance. method = "mrf" now requires K >= 2 (one-state Potts field degenerate). augmented Normal-Gamma routes remain explicitly blocked pending batch 2 (planned mixing benchmark direct-t routes). Recovery harness gains mrfdist1 mrfdist1reg groups: six new combinations pass synthetic two-block lattice across 3 seeds (parameter recovery + allocation accuracy 0.98-1.00 verification runs).

what: Spatial line hardening + official-statistics case study
title: nimix 0.8.0
text: usStateAdj (state contiguity matrix derived Census Bureau’s 2023 county adjacency file); full provenance help pages. New vignette spatial-mixture.Rmd: 2023 SAIPE poverty rates MRF engine (estimated interaction, posterior mean ~1.2) finds two regimes whose high-poverty regime spatially contiguous Southern belt (AL AR DC FL GA LA MS NC NM OK SC TN TX) – structure exchangeable mixture represent; plain fixedk fit agrees 82% states. spatial regression example honestly collapses single national income-poverty regime. recovery harness gains mrfbeta group (pseudo-likelihood beta estimation synthetic lattice, 3 seeds).

what: MRF engine: Bayesian estimation of the interaction beta
title: nimix 0.7.0
text: prior$estimateBeta = TRUE (optional prior$betaMax, default 2) puts uniform prior Potts interaction updates random-walk Metropolis Besag (1975) pseudo-likelihood – classical approximate route hidden Potts fields, since exact posterior beta doubly intractable. documented approximation; exchange-algorithm refinement (Murray, Ghahramani & MacKay 2006) possible future upgrade. beta now model node modes; estimateBeta = FALSE (default) sampler touches , reproducing fixed-beta behaviour exactly. Correctness guards: NIMBLE’s default sampler beta (target unnormalised Potts density – wrong beta) removed unconditionally, compiled-model cache now distinguishes estimation fixed mode (compile different sampler sets). MRF-specific diagnostics: summary() reports split-Rhat / ESS posterior mean beta estimated, engine warns posterior piles near betaMax (near-saturated field). synthetic two-block lattice, estimated interaction concentrates well zero (posterior mean ~1.6, P(beta > 0.2) = 1) perfect block recovery, fixed-beta path unchanged.

title: nimix 0.7.0
text: nimixReg() gains method = "mrf" spatialWeights argument: mixture Gaussian linear regressions whose latent regime labels follow Potts field neighbourhood graph – regression coefficients cluster spatially (e.g. growth patterns across adjacent regions). fixed prior$beta interaction clustering engine. synthetic two-block lattice opposite slopes (+2 / -2) heavy noise, spatial smoothing lifts regime-allocation accuracy 0.867 (fixedk) 1.000 recovering slopes. recovery harness gains mrfreg group (3 seeds). Heavy-tailed regression responses explicitly blocked mrf clear message (inherit Gaussian regression spec otherwise use wrong emission density).

title: nimix 0.7.0
text: method = "mrf" now also accepts multivariate data: multivariate Gaussian components Normal-Inverse-Wishart kernel, label sweep evaluating dmnorm_chol per component (Cholesky factors hoisted per sweep). synthetic two-block lattice spatial smoothing lifts multivariate allocation accuracy 0.942 (fixedk) 0.992. Sampler selection fully polymorphic (S4 dispatch component spec); heavy-tailed families inherit Gaussian specs explicitly blocked clear message instead silently using wrong emission density.

what: Spatially constrained mixtures: the MRF engine
title: nimix 0.6.0
text: New method = "mrf" nimixClust(): finite mixture whose latent labels follow Potts Markov random field spatialWeights neighbourhood (Potts 1952; Besag 1974; spatially variant finite mixtures, Blekas et al. 2005), neighbouring observations favour component. Requires known K SpatialWeightSpec; interaction strength fixed prior$beta (default 0.8; beta = 0 removes smoothing). Univariate Gaussian components release. Implementation: (intentionally unnormalised) user-defined NIMBLE Potts distribution – exact MCMC beta fixed, intractable partition function constant – plus custom single-site Gibbs sweep sampler labels; theta updates remain conjugate. Bayesian estimation beta planned later 1.x release. synthetic two-block lattice overlapping components, spatial smoothing lifts allocation accuracy 0.883 (fixedk, smoothing) 0.967, beta = 0 reproduces unsmoothed behaviour. recovery harness gains mrf group (3 seeds, passing). Opens post-1.0 spatial line: spatially constrained mixtures latent component labels follow Markov random field neighbourhood graph (Besag 1974; spatially variant finite mixtures, Blekas et al. 2005). New S4 class SpatialWeightSpec: validated neighbourhood structure (symmetric, zero-diagonal, non-negative weight matrix) deliberately orthogonal DistributionSpec, registered component family can paired graph. Constructors spatialWeights() (matrix) gridAdjacency() (rook/queen contiguity regular lattice); accessors nRegions(), getAdjacency(), neighborsOf(). nimixClust() gains spatialWeights argument (default NULL, fully backward-compatible). Supplying structure validates points MRF engine planned 0.6.0; exchangeable mixture remains default behaviour. First stable release. public API – nimixClust(), nimixReg(), registerDistribution(), relabel(), summary(), plot(), predict(), nimixClearCache() – now considered stable; breaking changes bump major version.

title: nimix 0.6.0
text: New packaged dataset wdi2022: four development indicators 207 countries (World Bank World Development Indicators, 2022; CC 4.0; full provenance ?wdi2022). four vignettes now run official-statistics data instead simulations : income-regime clustering (univariate), joint income-longevity clustering (multivariate), Preston curve mixture regressions (instructive single-regime result: DPM invent components), Student-t vs Normal-Gamma heavy-tail comparison. Reported numbers vignettes come actual runs.

title: nimix 0.5.0
text: line opens performance hardening phase, built 0.4.3 feature set. two production engines method = "dpm" (Dirichlet process / Chinese restaurant process; number occupied components estimated) method = "fixedk" (finite mixture known K).

title: nimix 0.5.0
text: Compile-/ reuse. fit reuses compiled NIMBLE model (MCMC) later fit identical structure – generated code, constants, monitors component family – resetting data initial values. skips recompilation repeated fits (multiple seeds, multiple chains) bit--bit identical fresh compile. Controlled mcmcControl$reuse (default TRUE); nimixClearCache() releases cached compiled models. Reproducibility. dispersed (k-means) initialisation now seeded fit’s seed, repeated fits data seed coincide exactly. caller’s global random stream left untouched. Multi-chain + convergence diagnostics. mcmcControl$nchains (default 1) runs several chains dispersed, separately seeded starts, reusing compiled model first chain compiles. summary() reports rank-normalized split-Rhat effective sample size label-invariant quantities (occupied-cluster count, DPM concentration), warns Rhat exceeds 1.1. Per-component parameters deliberately excluded Rhat avoid label-switching artefacts. Vectorised post-processing. per-draw occupied-cluster count, allocation-trace parsing, relabelling recode/weight steps now vectorised (single tabulate/matrix-index passes instead per-row loops). Verified bit--bit identical previous implementation; remaining relabelling cost external ECR algorithm . Hardening harness. inst/harness/run_recovery_suite.R now systematic distribution x engine recovery matrix: every released clustering family (Gaussian, Student-t, Normal-Gamma – univariate multivariate – Poisson, Binomial) regression path fitted engines data known truth, across three MCMC seeds (18 combinations, 54 fits). DPM discrete counts recovery criterion assesses dominant components (weight >= 0.1), reflecting known diffuseness DPM posterior number components data (Miller & Harrison, 2013). Previously untested engine pairings additionally pinned tests/testthat/test-hardening-matrix.R.

title: nimix 0.4.3
text: verbose now defaults FALSE nimixClust() nimixReg(). Benign NIMBLE configuration chatter (e.g. Chinese-restaurant-process truncation reminder) muffled selectively; genuine warnings potentially invalid MCMC draws errors always propagate, verbose modes. Dispersed k-means initialisation now headroom-aware: number seeded clusters respects truncation level (K_max) early CRP transients longer breach smaller K_max. initialisation headroom configurable mcmcControl$initRatio (default 0.8). Values leave little headroom (>= 0.95) warn; values outside (0, 1) error.

title: nimix 0.4.3
text: Two production engines: method = "dpm" method = "fixedk", available univariate multivariate clustering regression (including multivariate responses). Component distributions: Gaussian (univariate / multivariate), Student-t Normal-Gamma (heavy-tailed, univariate / multivariate), Poisson / Binomial counts.

title: nimix 0.4.2
text: nimixReg() gains multivariate responses (cbind(y1, y2) ~ x) Normal, Student-t Normal-Gamma components, per-component coefficient matrices error covariances.

title: nimix 0.4.0
text: Student-t Normal-Gamma components (univariate multivariate) Poisson / Binomial counts; public registerDistribution().

title: nimix 0.3.0
text: nimixReg() RegressionMixModel class: mixture--regressions Normal-Inverse-Gamma g-prior. FixedKEngine implemented across univariate, multivariate regression models. Engine selection polymorphic via runEngine().

title: nimix 0.2.0
text: Multivariate Gaussian clustering (NormalMvSpec) Normal-Inverse-Wishart base measure. Engine generalised dimension-agnostic.

title: nimix 0.1.0
text: S4 foundation, univariate Gaussian clustering (NormalUvSpec), nimixClust() DPM fixed-K engines.
